facts_disclosed: Some college at HCC, dropped out to work

turns.content: new year new grind bro. back to studying for this real estate exam
Happy new year! Good to hear you're hitting the books again. How's the studying going so far?
slow honestly. i do it between rides and at night when ismail is asleep. but im making progress
Progress is progress, even if it's slow. Fitting studying around a full work schedule is no joke. Have you done any formal classes for it, or are you self-studying?
self study bro. i tried the school thing before it didnt work out for me
Nothing wrong with that — self-study lets you go at your own pace. What happened with school before, if you don't mind me asking?
i did some classes at hcc. houston community college. but i couldnt keep it up. working full time paying bills and trying to go to class its too much bro something had to give
That's a brutal combination. Full-time work plus bills plus classes — something always has to give, and you can't exactly stop paying bills. How far did you get before you had to step back?
like a year maybe. got some credits but it wasnt leading anywhere fast enough. i needed money now not a degree in 4 years
That's real. When you've got immediate financial responsibilities, a four-year timeline doesn't match the urgency. At least you gave it a shot and now you know what works better for you.
thats why the real estate license is different. i can study on my own time no schedule no tuition. just pass the exam and go
Exactly — it's a clear, practical path. Study, pass, start earning. No semesters, no waiting around. It fits your life way better than the traditional school route.
wallahi bro. and i already know the city from driving. so its not like im starting from nothing i just need the paper
You've basically been doing field research for three years without even realizing it. The license is just making it official. What sections of the exam are giving you the most trouble?
the legal stuff bro. contracts and regulations. the actual property knowledge im good on but the law parts are dry

Yeah, the legal and contract sections are where most people struggle — it's a lot of memorization and specific language. Have you tried practice exams? They help you figure out how the questions are actually worded, which is half the battle.
yeah i do them on my phone in the car when im waiting for rides lol. getting better at it. inshallah i pass first try
Studying on your phone between rides — that's hustle right there. Inshallah you nail it first try. You're putting in the work, it's going to pay off.
